draft.introduction: The Devi Mahatmya (Glory of the Goddess) is the foundational text of Shakta Hinduism, narrating the Goddess’s supreme power in defeating demons that the male gods could not overcome. It establishes the feminine divine as the ultimate cosmic force.

translation.text: When the great demon Mahishasura conquered the three worlds and usurped the throne of Indra, the gods, unable to defeat him, combined their fiery energies (tejas). From this concentrated radiance emerged the Goddess Durga, resplendent and invincible, bearing weapons given by each god. She alone would destroy what none of them could.

translator_notes.note: The Goddess emerging from the combined energies of the male gods inverts the typical creation pattern: the feminine principle is not derivative but the supreme concentration of all divine power. This text revolutionized Hindu theology by establishing the Goddess as not merely consort but as the ultimate reality (Brahman) in feminine form.
